zh.Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland: 蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾布臣,第一代特威茲穆爾男爵
zh.Badenoch, Highland, Scotland: 英國蘇格蘭希倫德巴登
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Comyn Lord Of Badenoch於1194年出生在蘇格蘭艾爾金爾時，他的父親William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan31歲，母親Sarah FitzHugh28歲。他於1215年與Eve Amabilia de Galloway在蘇格蘭音巴內-爾巴登結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1249年7月在蘇格蘭音巴內-爾巴登享年55歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭音巴內-爾巴登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Comyn Lord Of Badenoch于1194年出生在苏格兰艾爾金爾时，他的父亲William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan31岁，母亲Sarah FitzHugh28岁。他于1215年与Eve Amabilia de Galloway在苏格兰音巴內-爾巴登结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1249年7月在苏格兰音巴內-爾巴登享年55岁，埋葬在苏格兰音巴內-爾巴登。

nameMeanings.name: Comyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Cumming
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): most probably a habitational name from Bosc-Benard-Commin in Eure, France. This place lies on the edge of the Forêt de Rouvray, near Rouen, in the heart of the district from which came numerous Domesday barons, and so is much more likely to be the source of the name than Comines in Nord. The name is also possibly derived from the personal name Cumin, which is found in Norfolk, Lincolnshire, and Yorkshire in the 12th and 13th centuries and may be of Breton origin, according to Reaney. Compare Old Breton Cunmin, which also appears as Cumin, and is from con- ‘hound’ with an uncertain second element, perhaps the min ‘edge’ which is found in other personal names.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Cuimín (or Ó Cuimín) ‘son (or ‘descendant’) of Cuimín’, a personal name formed from a diminutive of cam ‘crooked’. There was a 7th-century abbot of Iona named Cumin. In Scotland, this Irish surname became confused with the Norman name.

History: A notable bearer of the Norman surname is Wilhelmus Comyn, Bishop of Durham, who came north to Scotland with King David I in 1124 and was appointed Chancellor of Scotland. He established his nephew Richard in Tynedale and Roxburghshire, and Richard also rose to be Chancellor of Scotland. The Comyn or Cumming family gained the earldoms of Buchan and Menteith and rose to be the most powerful noble family in Scotland in the century before the rise of King Robert Bruce.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Badenoch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Scottish: locative name from province of Badenoch in Inverness.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Comyn Lord Of Badenoch was born in 1194, in Elginshire, Scotland, his father, William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan, was 31 and his mother, Sarah FitzHugh, was 28. He married Eve Amabilia de Galloway in 1215, in Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in July 1249, in Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland, at the age of 55, and was buried in Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Comyn Lord Of Badenoch est né en 1194, à Elginshire, Écosse, son père, William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Sarah FitzHugh, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Eve Amabilia de Galloway en 1215, à Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en juillet 1249, à Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Écosse, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Comyn Lord Of Badenoch 1194 in Elginshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan 31 und seine Mutter Sarah FitzHugh 28. Er hat 1215 in Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland Eve Amabilia de Galloway geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb im Juli 1249 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland und wurde in Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Comyn Lord Of Badenoch è nato il nel 1194 nel Elginshire, Scotland, il suo padre, William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Sarah FitzHugh, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Eve Amabilia de Galloway nel 1215, nel Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel luglio 1249, nel Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolto a Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Comyn Lord Of Badenochが1194年にスコットランドエルジンシュアイアで生まれた時，父William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchanは31歳，母Sarah FitzHughは28歳であった。1215年にスコットランドインバーネス-シュアイアバデノチでEve Amabilia de Gallowayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1249年7月にスコットランドインバーネス-シュアイアバデノチで55歳で死去し，スコットランドインバーネス-シュアイアバデノチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Comyn Lord Of Badenoch(이)가 1194년 스컷랜드 엘긴실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Sarah FitzHugh(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1215년 스컷랜드 인버네스-실르 바데노치에서 Eve Amabilia de Galloway(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1249년 7월 스컷랜드 인버네스-실르 바데노치에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 인버네스-실르 바데노치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Comyn Lord Of Badenoch nasceu em 1194, em Elginshire, Scotland, seu pai, William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Sarah FitzHugh, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Eve Amabilia de Galloway em 1215, em Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em julho de 1249, em Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland, com 55 anos, e foi sepultado em Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Comyn Lord Of Badenoch родился в 1194 г. в местности элгиншир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени William Comyn Lord of Badenoch and Earl Of Buchan было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Sarah FitzHugh, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Eve Amabilia de Galloway в 1215 г. в местности баденох, инвирнесс-шир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер июль 1249 г. в местности баденох, инвирнесс-шир, Шотландия в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности баденох, инвирнесс-шир, Шотландия.

Se casó con Eve Amabilia de Galloway en 1215, en Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en julio de 1249, en Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultado en Badenoch, Inverness-shire, Scotland.
